briefs.title: Council to Consider: National Day of Mourning Presentation by Labour Council
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to hear a presentation from the New Westminster and District Labour Council on the National Day of Mourning. This day, observed on April 28, honours workers who have been killed, injured, or made ill on the job — a moment relevant to all business owners with employees.
briefs.action_required: Note April 28 as the National Day of Mourning — consider acknowledging it with your employees.

key_dates.label: National Day of Mourning — Maple Ridge Council presentation

briefs.action_required: Review the Maple Ridge Council agenda for April 28, 2026 and submit your comments before the meeting.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Maple Ridge Reconsideration Item — April 28, 2026
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to revisit a previous decision under a formal reconsideration process — meaning councillors will vote on whether to reopen and potentially change an earlier ruling. The specific item under reconsideration is not detailed in the agenda excerpt, so business owners in Maple Ridge should monitor the full agenda for details that may affect their operations.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge Council reconsideration vote — public meeting date

briefs.title: Council to Vote on Upholding Refusal of 6-Storey Apartment Near Haney Slide
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on whether to uphold a planning director's decision to refuse a development permit for a 6-storey apartment building at 22190 Cliff Avenue. The property sits near the historic Haney Slide — a major landslide zone — and city-hired experts say the applicant's safety reports still don't adequately prove the site is safe to build on.
briefs.action_required: If you own or invest in property near the Fraser River Escarpment, watch this decision closely.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge Council vote — appeal of development permit refusal, 22190 Cliff Avenue

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Maple Ridge Consent Agenda Items — April 28, 2026
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on receiving the items listed in the April 28, 2026 Regular Council Consent Agenda into the official record. A consent agenda is a package of routine items grouped together for a single vote — no specific policy changes or business impacts have been detailed in this notice.

briefs.title: Council to receive report on Maple Ridge Q1 2026 contract awards over $750,000
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to receive a report listing six contracts — each worth more than $750,000 — that were approved by the Chief Administrative Officer (CAO) between January 1 and March 31, 2026. The contracts cover items such as a wildland fire truck, fuel supply, software licenses, road consulting, park improvements, and a reservoir replacement, totalling over $9 million.
briefs.action_required: No action required — this is an information-only report with no direct impact on business owners.

briefs.title: Council to consider receiving Maple Ridge Council expenses report for March 2026
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on receiving its expense report for March 2026 for information. The report lists expenses that councillors are required to disclose publicly each year under the Statement of Financial Information (SOFI) — a provincial rule that makes government spending transparent — and reflects an updated expense policy that now includes costs for cell phones and devices used for council work.

briefs.title: Council to vote on repealing Kanaka Business Park landscape maintenance tax levy
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on repealing a bylaw that has charged property owners at Kanaka Business Park (Lots 1–51) a parcel tax — a fee tied to your property — to pay for shared landscape maintenance since 2009. A valid petition signed by more than 50% of affected owners has been certified, and Council will now consider whether to scrap the levy entirely.
briefs.action_required: If you own property at Kanaka Business Park (Lots 1–51), watch for the Council decision — it directly affects your tax bill.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge Council vote — Kanaka Business Park landscape levy repeal bylaw

briefs.title: Council to vote on allowing animal shelter use on Maple Ridge agricultural property
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on a proposed zoning bylaw (No. 8100-2026) that would allow SAINTS — a non-profit sanctuary for senior and special-needs animals — to operate an animal shelter at 10950 284 Street. The property is zoned A-1 (Small Holding Agricultural) and the bylaw change would apply only to this specific property, letting SAINTS use the existing house as office and animal care space while also building a new barn.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 28, 2026 Council meeting if you want to observe or comment before the vote.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge Zone Amending Bylaw No. 8100-2026 Council vote — adoption hearing

briefs.title: Proposed: Maple Ridge Council to vote on allowing pharmacy at new Lougheed Highway medical clinic
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow a pharmacy to operate inside a new doctor's office at 21783 Lougheed Highway. The proposal would also remove a legal restriction (called a restrictive covenant) that currently bans a pharmacy at that address — a restriction that was put in place during an earlier rezoning due to concerns about too many pharmacies in the area.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it could affect pharmacy or healthcare business opportunities on Lougheed Highway.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge Zone Amending Bylaw No. 8090-2026 — first, second, third reading council vote

briefs.title: Proposed: Maple Ridge to vote on stronger tree protection and removal rules
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on a new Tree Protection & Management Bylaw that would replace the current 2015 bylaw. The proposed bylaw would require a stated reason before any protected tree (20cm diameter or larger) can be removed, increase the number of replacement trees required when removals are approved, and introduce new fees designed to discourage removing multiple trees at once.
briefs.action_required: If your business involves tree removal or land development in Maple Ridge, review the proposed bylaw before Council votes on April 28, 2026.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge Tree Protection & Management Bylaw No. 8045-2026 — Council vote (first, second, third readings)

briefs.title: Proposed: Maple Ridge to Update Tree Permit Fees for Homeowners and Developers
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the fees charged when someone applies to remove trees on their property. A proposed bylaw would create two separate fee streams — one for homeowners and one for developers — and add new fees for things like cash payments instead of planting replacement trees ($800 per tree) and re-inspection visits ($150 each).
briefs.action_required: If you plan to remove trees in Maple Ridge, review the proposed new fee schedule before Council votes on April 28, 2026.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge Tree Permit Fees Amending Bylaw No. 8103-2026 — Council vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Council to vote on Maple Ridge 2026–2030 Financial Plan with 3.5% property tax increase
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on the 2026–2030 Financial Plan Bylaw, which would lock in the city's full budget for the next five years. If approved, the plan would mean a 3.5% property tax increase in 2026 — about $101 more per year for a home valued at $1.04 million — rising to 4.5% annually from 2027 to 2030.
briefs.action_required: Check mapleridge.ca/budget to see how the proposed spending plan may affect your business costs.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge 2026–2030 Financial Plan Bylaw adoption deadline — Municipal

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on Maple Ridge 2026 property tax rates — 3.5% municipal increase proposed
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on a bylaw that would set property tax rates for 2026. The proposed bylaw includes a 3.5% municipal tax increase — described as one of the lowest in the region. Your actual tax bill may differ from that 3.5% figure depending on how your property's assessed value changed compared to the city-wide average.
briefs.action_required: Check your 2026 property assessment to estimate how your tax bill may change if this bylaw passes.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge 2026 Property Tax Rates Bylaw — council adoption deadline

briefs.title: Council to vote on Maple Ridge Strategic Plan Q1 2026 progress update
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on approving a quarterly progress report on its 2023–2026 Strategic Plan. The update highlights completed goals — including RCMP de-integration and film industry milestones — and proposes adding new targets tied to the Resilient Future 2050 Climate Action Plan, covering tree planting, agricultural support, flood and heat preparedness, and business retention efforts.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new climate and business targets could affect your sector.
